scoreability.reason: Union leave terms are concrete but mostly unpaid and subgroup/role-specific, with service requirements and caps.

review_notes: Also informs C_UNION_ACCESS_BUSINESS but should not be double-counted as separate paid leave generosity without subobject handling.

CBA_04.concept_label: Personal, educational, union, political, religious, sabbatical, and other named leave

review_notes: Score, if used, should reflect protected unpaid/service-credit increment, not statutory floor alone.

CBA_04.concept_label: Layoff order, seniority, bumping, and reduction-in-force protections

subgroup_terms.subgroup_label: Employees with more than 12 months service
subgroup_terms.terms: May elect termination allowance, voluntary layoff with recall, or transfer/bumping rights; bumping scope depends on service tier.

subgroup_terms.subgroup_label: Regular employees with <=12 months or part-time less than 12 months
subgroup_terms.terms: Inverse accredited service followed only if other factors equal; management essential-skill discretion retained.

review_notes: ISP voluntary termination occurs before Article 9 in defined circumstances and is recorded under severance/income security.

scoreability.reason: Recall duration, order, notice/report conditions, and consequence rules are CBA-contained.

CBA_06.concept_label: Vacation and annual leave

CBA_06.concept_label: Personal, educational, union, political, religious, sabbatical, and other named leave

excluded_groups: employees under six months service
employees below 20 scheduled hours per week

subgroup_terms.terms: 2 weeks after six months but under one year; 1 month after one to under five years; 8 weeks after more than five years.

CBA_07.beneficiary_or_affected_group: Eligible regular employees requesting personal leave

granularity_decision.reason: Unpaid personal leave is distinct from paid PTO conversion and other named leave.

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Maximum period of personal leave shall be... two weeks... one month... eight weeks.
evidence.heading: SECTION 13(B)

fields.value: same job and schedule if leave no longer than two months; same job classification and work schedule if longer than two months

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Assured of return to same job and schedule... no longer than two months... longer than two months... same job classification and work schedule.
evidence.heading: SECTION 13(B)

fields.value: granted at discretion of Employer; Employer agrees not to act arbitrarily or capriciously

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Reasonable unpaid personal Leaves... at the discretion of the Employer... Employer agrees not to act in an arbitrary or capricious manner.
evidence.heading: SECTION 13(B),(K)

fields.coverage_check: Discretion is a major score limit.

scoreability.reason: Unpaid personal leave has duration and return protection, but is employer-discretionary and limited to 20+ scheduled-hour regular employees.

bridge_score.explanation: Narrow/modest personal leave: unpaid and discretionary, but with service-tier durations and return/seniority protection.

review_notes: Do not merge with PTO conversion; this is unpaid personal leave.

CBA_07.concept_label: Paid sick leave
CBA_07.covered_group: Regular employees working 20+ hours per week, employed more than six months, with 80 hours in prior month and statutory disability/workers compensation eligibility.

population_scope.scope_label: regular 20+ hour employees meeting service/hours/statutory conditions
excluded_groups: non-regular employees
regular employees under 20 hours/week
employees without required service/hours except stated waiver

subgroup_terms.terms: Waiting period and maximum weeks vary by years of continuous employment.

CBA_07.beneficiary_or_affected_group: Eligible employees unable to work because of sickness or injury

granularity_decision.reason: Waiting-period and benefit-duration tiers are variants inside one sick-pay concept.

fields.value: regular 20+ hour employee, more than six months employed, 80 hours in prior month, qualified for SDI or workers compensation

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Regular employee who works 20 hours or more per week... employed for more than six months and worked eighty hours... has applied for and qualified for State Disability Insurance or Workers Compensation.
evidence.heading: SECTION 29 - SICKNESS PAY BENEFITS

fields.value: 8, 16, or 26 work weeks by service tier

evidence.quote_or_pointer: One year or more but less than two years eight weeks; two to less than five years sixteen weeks; five years or more twenty-six weeks.
evidence.heading: SECTION 29(D)

fields.value: 66 2/3% of average pay less California Disability Insurance or Workers Compensation benefits
fields.unit: percent of average pay less offsets

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Sickness pay benefits shall be paid at sixty-six and two thirds of average pay... less benefits received from California Disability Insurance... or Workers Compensation.
evidence.heading: SECTION 29(E)

fields.coverage_check: CBA supplement formula; statutory benefits are offsets.

fields.value: medical certification and disability claim documentation required; failure within six days may delay/loss benefits

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Provide a medical certification... within six calendar days... may result in loss or delay in payment... No sickness pay until claim filed and certification on file.
evidence.heading: SECTION 29(F)

scoreability.reason: CBA-contained sick pay supplement is substantial but eligibility is narrow, integrated with statutory benefits, has waiting periods and documentation burden.

bridge_score.explanation: Strong paid sick/disability income supplement for eligible regular employees: up to 26 weeks at 66 2/3% average pay less offsets, with service/hour and documentation limits.

review_notes: Do not treat SDI/workers compensation as the CBA benefit; CBA supplement is scored.

CBA_07.concept_label: Parental, family, maternity, adoption, and FMLA-type leave
CBA_07.covered_group: Employees eligible under FMLA/CFRA/PDL and incorporated Macy's leave policies.

population_scope.scope_basis: Appendix D FMLA/CFRA/PDL terms incorporated by Section 13
excluded_groups: employees lacking 12 months/1250 hours for FMLA/CFRA
worksites not meeting 50 employees within 75 miles for FMLA/CFRA

subgroup_terms.terms: PDL up to four months/88 workdays for full-time employee; transfer to less strenuous/hazardous duties if medically advisable and reasonably accommodated.
Up to 12 weeks unpaid leave in rolling 12-month period for family/medical reasons with restoration rules.

CBA_07.beneficiary_or_affected_group: Eligible employees needing family, medical, bonding, pregnancy, or related leave

granularity_decision.reason: FMLA, CFRA, and PDL are related family/parental/medical leave variants in incorporated Appendix D.

fields.value: up to 12 weeks unpaid leave within rolling 12-month period

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Eligible to take up to 12 weeks of unpaid Family and Medical Leave within any 12-month period... rolling 12-month period.
evidence.heading: APPENDIX D - FMLA LEAVE POLICY

fields.value: up to four months or 88 workdays for full-time employee per pregnancy

evidence.quote_or_pointer: PDL... up to four months (or 88 workdays for a full-time employee) per pregnancy.
evidence.heading: CALIFORNIA PREGNANCY DISABILITY LEAVE

fields.value: Most leave time is unpaid; paid vacation/PTO may be substituted but does not extend leave and cannot exceed 100% salary.

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Most leave time is unpaid... substitution of paid leave time... does not extend the 12-week leave period... not more than 100% salary.
evidence.heading: APPENDIX D

fields.coverage_check: No separate CBA-paid parental leave identified.

fields.value: health benefits maintained for first 12 weeks of approved Family and Medical Leave; scored separately as benefit continuation

evidence.quote_or_pointer: For the first 12 weeks of an approved Family and Medical Leave, the Company will maintain your health benefits as if actively employed.
evidence.heading: MEDICAL AND OTHER BENEFITS

rejected_values.candidate_value: up to 6 weeks partially paid leave under Paid Family Care Leave Act
rejected_values.reason_rejected: The text references statutory Family Temporary Disability Insurance/benefit brochure, not a CBA-paid parental leave entitlement.

scoreability.reason: Appendix mostly restates statutory/policy FMLA/CFRA/PDL terms and does not clearly add CBA-specific paid parental/family leave beyond vacation/PTO substitution and separately recorded benefit continuation.
missing_or_external_inputs: clear contractual increment beyond statutory leave

bridge_score.explanation: Withheld under statutory-baseline rule; benefit continuation scored separately.

review_notes: Central review should decide whether Appendix D incorporation itself is a contractual increment; I withheld scalar score.

CBA_07.concept_label: Layoff order, seniority, bumping, and reduction-in-force protections
CBA_07.covered_group: Covered employees with seniority rights, with seasonal/orientation/facility/classification variants.

excluded_groups: Seasonal employees lack recall rights
orientation-period employees have limited rights

subgroup_terms.terms: Layoff by Burns list seniority before reducing base hours.
Layoff by seniority before reducing base hours.
Seniority-based displacement of least senior same-classification employee if merit/ability sufficient; in-store training and hiring freeze.

CBA_07.beneficiary_or_affected_group: Employees affected by layoff or reduction in force

granularity_decision.reason: Layoff notice, seniority order, bumping/displacement and subgroup Burns list rules are variants inside layoff-order concept.

fields.value: 3 days for Regular Employees; 24 hours/end preceding workday for orientation-period, Fine Jewelry Center permanent layoffs, or Seasonal Employees

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Three days notice... before laying off Regular Employees... 24 hours prior... for Orientation Period, Fine Jewelry Center permanent layoffs or Seasonal Employees.
evidence.heading: SECTION 8(J)

fields.value: seniority controls layoff/rehiring where merit and ability are equal; specific Burns/sales seniority rules before base-hour reductions

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Seniority shall prevail and control in such layoffs or rehiring where... merit and ability are equal; Employer agrees to layoff by Burns list seniority... lay-off by seniority prior to reducing base hours.
evidence.heading: SECTION 16 / SECTION 8

fields.coverage_check: Employer merit/ability judgment is a limit.

fields.value: displaced employees offered opportunity by seniority to displace least senior employee in same classification if qualified; training required for available positions

evidence.quote_or_pointer: In order of seniority, offered the opportunity to displace among the least senior employee in same classification... Employer shall provide whatever in-store training necessary.
evidence.heading: SECTION 35

rejected_values.reason_rejected: Minimum headcount is staffing/schedule context, not a layoff-order value.

scoreability.reason: Objective seniority/order, notice, and displacement/bumping protections are supported, but order is conditioned on merit/ability equality and subgroup rules vary.

bridge_score.explanation: Strong layoff-order protections through seniority, notice, Burns-list subgroup rule, bumping/displacement and training/hiring freeze, tempered by short notice and merit/ability discretion.

review_notes: Appendix E OCR used only as corroborating subgroup context.

CBA_07.concept_label: Recall, rehire, and preferential hiring rights
CBA_07.covered_group: Employees entitled to recall from layoff; seasonal employees excluded and orientation-period employees limited.

excluded_groups: Seasonal employees have no recall rights
some orientation-period employees have limited recall before new hires for up to four months

subgroup_terms.terms: When laid off, excluding seasonal employees, offered qualified employment before new hires for up to four months unemployment.
Employer offers future available positions to layoff-status employees from job elimination before new hires, under Section 16 and with Section 35 priority over Section 17E.

granularity_decision.reason: Recall notice, seniority retention, and transfer/removal preferential hiring are recall/rehire variants.

fields.value: telephone/family notification or written communication to last home address where employee cannot be contacted by telephone

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Where an Employee is entitled to recall... and cannot be contacted by telephone, Employer will notify... by written communication... Telephone notification to family... fulfills requirements.
evidence.heading: SECTION 16(D)

fields.value: seniority terminates after 4/6/9/12 consecutive months unemployment by service tier; failure to return within 3 working days of certified-mail recall forfeits seniority absent just cause

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Under six months service, four consecutive months; six months to one year, six months; one to two years, nine months; two or more years, twelve months... failure to return within three working days.
evidence.heading: SECTION 16(E)

fields.value: positions becoming available in future offered to layoff-status employees before any new employee is hired

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Employer agrees to offer to those on layoff status, as a result of the job elimination, positions that become available in the future before any new employee is hired.
evidence.heading: SECTION 35

scoreability.reason: Recall duration, notice and preferential hiring are supported, but durations vary by service and seasonal/orientation exclusions matter.

bridge_score.explanation: Strong recall/rehire protection for regular employees: ordered notice and up to 12 months seniority retention, plus job-elimination preferential hiring, with exclusions and forfeiture traps.

review_notes: Recall distinct from layoff order.

CBA_07.concept_label: Severance, separation pay, and income-security benefits
CBA_07.covered_group: Employees displaced by transfer/removal of work that results in layoff.

excluded_groups: ordinary layoffs not caused by transfer/removal displacement are not covered by a severance formula

CBA_07.beneficiary_or_affected_group: Displaced employees whose loss of work results in layoff

granularity_decision.reason: Only one severance-related framework appears; no formula to split.

fields.value: Employer and Union will meet to discuss effects of layoff and negotiate applicable severance and general release if displacement causes layoff

evidence.quote_or_pointer: If displacement results in loss of work resulting in layoff, then Employer and Union will meet to discuss the effects of such layoff and negotiate applicable severance and general release.
evidence.heading: SECTION 35

rejected_values.reason_rejected: Notice to Union before transfer/removal of work is not a severance payment formula.

scoreability.reason: CBA requires effects discussion and severance negotiation in a specific transfer/removal layoff scenario but states no severance formula, cap, payment timing, or eligibility entitlement.

bridge_score.explanation: Withheld as framework-only severance negotiation process.

review_notes: Do not score absence as low; only framework evidence exists.

CBA_07.concept_label: Benefit continuation during layoff, closure, leave, or disability
CBA_07.covered_group: Employees on approved Family and Medical Leave, including pregnancy disability followed by newborn-care leave.

population_scope.scope_basis: Appendix D Medical and Other Benefits
excluded_groups: employees not on approved FMLA/CFRA-type leave

subgroup_terms.subgroup_label: pregnancy disability followed by newborn-care leave
subgroup_terms.terms: Leave may exceed 12 weeks, but company medical contribution paid only for first 12 weeks.

granularity_decision.reason: Health benefit continuation during leave is distinct from active medical plan design and parental leave duration.

fields.value: health benefits/medical benefits

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Company will maintain your health benefits as if you continued to be actively employed.
evidence.heading: MEDICAL AND OTHER BENEFITS

fields.value: first 12 weeks of approved Family and Medical Leave

evidence.quote_or_pointer: For the first 12 weeks of an approved Family and Medical Leave, the Company will maintain your health benefits.
evidence.heading: MEDICAL AND OTHER BENEFITS

fields.value: Company pays company contribution for first 12 weeks; employee pays employee premium portion by payroll deduction or direct payment; late/nonreturn reimbursement rules apply.

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Company will only pay the company contribution... first 12 weeks... deduct your portion... or direct payment... reimburse Company for premiums if not returning for at least 30 days.
evidence.heading: MEDICAL AND OTHER BENEFITS

scoreability.reason: Continuation duration, benefit type and payment responsibility are CBA-contained in Appendix D, but tied to statutory/policy family-medical leave and employee premium obligations.

bridge_score.explanation: Meaningful but limited benefit continuation: 12 weeks with company contribution, employee premium portion and reimbursement/late-payment conditions.

review_notes: Do not use as active medical plan-design generosity.

CBA_07.concept_label: Just cause and substantive discipline/discharge standard
CBA_07.covered_group: Covered employees, subject to orientation-period limits.

population_scope.scope_label: unit-wide discipline standard with probationary exclusion
excluded_groups: employees under three months generally not subject to Section 31 except union membership/activity terminations

subgroup_terms.terms: Terminations not subject to Section 31 except Union membership/activity.

granularity_decision.reason: Just-cause standard is distinct from progressive-discipline steps and grievance/arbitration process.

fields.value: discharge only for just cause

evidence.quote_or_pointer: The Employer shall have the right to discharge any employee for just cause.
evidence.heading: SECTION 15 DISCIPLINE OF EMPLOYEES

fields.value: Union representative must be present at discharge unless employee waives in writing

evidence.quote_or_pointer: A Union representative must be present at the time of discharge except where the Employee designates in writing they do not want Union representation.
evidence.heading: SECTION 15

fields.value: appeal discharge through Union within 10 calendar days from Union notice

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Right to appeal such discharge through the Union within ten calendar days from the date the Union receives notice.
evidence.heading: SECTION 15(A)

scoreability.reason: Just cause, representation, appeal route and arbitration interface are supported, but serious-offense exceptions and probationary exclusion limit coverage.

bridge_score.explanation: Strong just-cause discipline protection with representation and arbitral review of immediate discharge severity, limited by listed immediate-discharge offenses and orientation-period exclusion.

review_notes: Progressive discipline and investigation appeal split into separate records.

CBA_07.concept_label: Progressive discipline steps, warnings, and notice
CBA_07.covered_group: Covered employees, with Appendix C non-probationary attendance-policy scope.

excluded_groups: probationary employees for Appendix C grievance scope

subgroup_terms.terms: Cautionary, written, final warning, then possible discharge; point thresholds stated for written/final/termination.

granularity_decision.reason: General progressive discipline and attendance-specific point progression are variants of the same discipline-steps concept.

fields.value: For offenses other than listed severe offenses, warnings and/or suspension prior to termination unless severity warrants immediate discharge.

evidence.quote_or_pointer: For all other offenses, Employer shall use progressive discipline (warning(s) and/or suspension(s) prior to termination), unless offense... warrants immediate discharge.
evidence.heading: SECTION 15(A)

fields.value: cautionary warnings void after 1 year; written warnings after 2 years; most final warnings after 3 years

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Cautionary warnings null and void after one year; written warnings after two years; final warnings... after three years.
evidence.heading: SECTION 15(E)

fields.value: 10 points/12 months or 6 points/3 months written warning; 12 points or second 6-point quarter final warning; 14 points termination

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Ten points... Written Warning; Twelve points... Final Warning; Fourteen points... Termination.
evidence.heading: APPENDIX C - ATTENDANCE POLICY

scoreability.reason: Progressive discipline is clear and supplemented by Appendix C thresholds, but immediate-discharge exceptions and attendance/job-abandonment variants matter.

bridge_score.explanation: Strong progressive discipline package with general progressive standard, warning sunset rules, attendance point thresholds and Union notice, subject to severe-offense exceptions.

review_notes: Attendance Appendix C is incorporated and operative.

CBA_07.concept_label: Investigation rights, representation, appeal, and record correction
CBA_07.covered_group: Employees subject to discipline/discharge and Union representation rights.

excluded_groups: employees under three months have limited Section 31 rights

granularity_decision.reason: Investigation timing, notice, evidence review, and attendance-policy grievance rights are one investigation/appeal concept.

fields.value: Employer has 10 calendar days to investigate suspension that could lead to discharge; from eleventh day average pay owed until notice/outcome sent if no communication.

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Employer shall have ten calendar days to investigate... If on the eleventh day... not communicated... Employee will be paid average rate...
evidence.heading: SECTION 15(A)

fields.value: written discharge notice to employee and Union within 5 days; written warnings furnished immediately to employee and within 14 days to Union

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Notices of discharge... in writing... within five days... written warning... to Employee immediately and Union within fourteen days.
evidence.heading: SECTION 15(B),(D)

fields.value: Union representative may review evidence supplied by shopper/investigator/undercover employee on written demand through Union.

evidence.quote_or_pointer: If discharged on basis of evidence supplied by store shopper, investigator, or undercover employee, Union representative shall have right to review such evidence upon written demand.
evidence.heading: SECTION 15(C)

scoreability.reason: Investigation, notice and appeal rights are concrete, but some rights are trigger-specific and appeal is union-gated.

bridge_score.explanation: Strong investigation/appeal protections: written notices, Union representation/evidence review, pay remedy after delayed suspension investigation, and grievance route, with union-gated and trigger-specific limits.

review_notes: Remedy is pay during delayed investigation, not full make-whole for all discipline.

CBA_07.concept_label: Grievance steps, timelines, representation, and scope
CBA_07.covered_group: Employees whose rights under express CBA terms are adversely affected, through Union-filed grievance process.

excluded_groups: issues not covered by express CBA terms
employees under three months have limited Section 31 access except union membership/activity terminations

granularity_decision.reason: Grievance procedure is distinct from arbitration access after mediation.

fields.value: dispute over interpretation or application of express CBA term alleging violation that adversely affected employee rights

evidence.quote_or_pointer: A grievance is defined as a dispute over interpretation or application of an express term... alleging agreement violated and employee rights adversely affected.
evidence.heading: SECTION 31

fields.value: 60 days from occurrence for general grievance; 10 calendar days from Union discharge notice for discharge grievance

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Must be filed within sixty days... Grievances regarding a discharge must be submitted... within ten calendar days.
evidence.heading: SECTION 31(B)-(C)

fields.value: grievance heard within 30 days at weekly meeting; employer failure to respond within 14 days from original meeting causes compliance with requested remedy

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Within thirty days... heard at weekly grievance meeting... Failure by Employer to respond within fourteen calendar days... shall cause Employer to comply with remedy requested.
evidence.heading: SECTION 31(B),(E)

scoreability.reason: Grievance process has clear scope, deadlines, meetings, representation path and default remedy, but access is Union-gated and procedural information burdens are strict.

bridge_score.explanation: Strong grievance procedure with 60-day filing, weekly meetings, mediation path and employer default remedy, tempered by express-term scope and Union/procedural gatekeeping.

review_notes: Arbitration fields scored separately.

CBA_07.concept_label: Arbitration access, selection, authority, costs, and finality
CBA_07.covered_group: Union-pursued grievances unresolved after weekly meetings and mediation.

population_scope.scope_label: grievances deadlocked after mediation and pursued by Union
excluded_groups: grievances not listed for mediation
grievances not timely demanded for arbitration
issues outside express CBA terms

CBA_07.beneficiary_or_affected_group: Employees whose grievances are advanced by the Union

granularity_decision.reason: Arbitration access is distinct from grievance filing and mediation steps.

fields.value: Union may request binding arbitration within 14 days after mediator written documentation, with list of issues

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Union may, within fourteen days following receipt of written documentation from Mediator, request in writing that matter be submitted to binding arbitration... list of issues.
evidence.heading: SECTION 31(I)

fields.value: arbitrator by party agreement or strike-off method from AAA list; each party may reject one list; AAA rules apply

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Arbitrator selected by agreement... if unable... strike-off method from list... AAA... each party right to reject one list.
evidence.heading: SECTION 31(J)

fields.value: arbitrator cannot add/subtract/change agreement; decision final and binding; costs split unless mediator-rejection prevailing-party rule shifts verified costs

evidence.quote_or_pointer: No power to add to, subtract from, change, or modify... decision final and binding... costs of prevailing party borne by other if mediator recommendation refused and result not more favorable; otherwise half each.
evidence.heading: SECTION 31(K)-(M)

scoreability.reason: Neutral binding arbitration is supported, but access is Union-gated after mediation, strict waiver rules apply, remedies are authority-limited, and cost shifting may burden the losing party.

bridge_score.explanation: Ordinary arbitration: binding neutral AAA path and finality, but union-gated access, waiver traps, authority limits and cost-shifting risk keep it around score 3.

review_notes: Aligned with G10 ordinary arbitration anchor.

CBA_07.concept_label: Active-worker medical eligibility, premium contribution, and worker cost-sharing terms
CBA_07.covered_group: Eligible employees covered by the agreement and qualified dependents.

excluded_groups: employees not eligible under external/majority plan rules

granularity_decision.reason: Health contribution/rate parity and eligibility references are one active-medical contribution layer; plan design split separately.

fields.value: contribution_cost_sharing

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Any changes in applicable health plan contribution rates or eligibility qualifications affecting majority... shall be effective at same time for employees covered under this Agreement.
evidence.heading: SECTION 28

fields.coverage_check: Contribution-rate parity is CBA-contained; exact rates external.

fields.value: eligible employees covered by agreement and qualified dependents

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Employer shall provide a health plan(s) for eligible employees covered by this Agreement and for qualified dependents.
evidence.heading: SECTION 28

fields.coverage_check: Eligibility population stated but detailed qualifications external.

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Health care plans outlined in booklets; contribution rates change with majority plan.
evidence.heading: SECTION 28

fields.coverage_check: Exact premium shares/payroll deductions not in CBA.

scoreability.reason: CBA states plan existence and contribution-rate/eligibility parity, but exact worker premium shares, employer contributions, payroll deductions, and eligibility qualifications are in external plan/booklet/majority-employee materials.

bridge_score.explanation: Withheld; active contribution layer has only external-profile/parity terms, not scoreable contribution amounts or worker burden.

review_notes: Contribution-cost-sharing layer separated from plan design; no externality-as-low score.

CBA_07.concept_label: Active-worker medical plan design, covered services, deductibles, copays, OOP limits, and dependent coverage
CBA_07.covered_group: Eligible employees and qualified dependents under the health plan.

excluded_groups: employees/dependents not qualified under external plan rules

granularity_decision.reason: Active medical plan design is a separate score layer from contribution/rate parity.

fields.value: external_profile

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Health care plan(s) shall be outlined in booklets which will be given each covered employee.
evidence.heading: SECTION 28

fields.coverage_check: Booklets external to CBA contain plan design.

evidence.quote_or_pointer: CBA provides no deductible, copay, coinsurance, OOP, prescription, covered-service, or plan-option terms in Section 28.
evidence.heading: SECTION 28

rejected_values.reason_rejected: This identifies possible dependent eligibility but not plan-design generosity, dependent premium, or coverage terms.

scoreability.reason: Plan design lives in external booklets/majority plan documents; CBA contains no substantive plan-design fields.

bridge_score.explanation: Withheld as external active medical plan design.

review_notes: Do not import leave-premium continuation terms or pension/sick values as active medical design.

CBA_07.concept_label: Life and AD&D insurance
CBA_07.covered_group: Unclear; Section title mentions life insurance but operative text discusses health plans only.

population_scope.scope_basis: Section 28 heading only for life insurance

granularity_decision.reason: The heading includes life insurance, but no operative life/AD&D benefit terms are recovered.

fields.value: Heading says Health and Life Insurance Plan, but body text recovered only health plan terms and no life/AD&D amount, eligibility, or formula.

evidence.quote_or_pointer: SECTION 28 - HEALTH AND LIFE INSURANCE PLAN; body: Employer shall provide a health plan(s)...
evidence.heading: SECTION 28 - HEALTH AND LIFE INSURANCE PLAN

fields.coverage_check: Heading-only evidence cannot support accepted life benefit fields.

rejected_values.candidate_value: SECTION 28 - HEALTH AND LIFE INSURANCE PLAN
rejected_values.reason_rejected: Heading mentions life insurance but no operative amount, coverage or eligibility is stated in body text.

scoreability.reason: Heading-only life-insurance signal lacks operative terms; retained only for central review of possible OCR omission.

bridge_score.explanation: Withheld due heading-only/ambiguous evidence.

review_notes: No accepted life/AD&D value.

CBA_07.concept_label: Pension and retirement plan eligibility/contributions
CBA_07.covered_group: Eligible employees covered under the CBA.

population_scope.scope_label: eligible employees under external pension plan
excluded_groups: employees not eligible under external plan rules

granularity_decision.reason: Only an external plan parity record is present.

fields.value: pension plan and benefits consistent with majority Macy's West employees

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Macy's West will provide a pension plan and pension benefits... consistent with the pension plan and benefits provided to the majority of Macy's West employees.
evidence.heading: SECTION 30 - PENSION

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Plan and benefits shall change when changes are made in the plan affecting majority employees; no benefit formula in CBA.
evidence.heading: SECTION 30 - PENSION

scoreability.reason: CBA names pension plan parity but gives no benefit formula, contribution rate, vesting or eligibility detail needed for pension score.

bridge_score.explanation: Withheld as external pension plan.

review_notes: No proxy score assigned.

CBA_07.concept_label: Base wage or salary rate
CBA_07.covered_group: Employees in selling, non-selling, general utility and other classifications in Section 33 wage tables.

subgroup_terms.terms: Dated thereafter rates by grade/location plus commission/draw arrangements.
Dated thereafter rates for grades A through I and F1BN etc.

granularity_decision.reason: Classification/location/date wage table variants are kept inside one wage base-rate record for central normalization.

fields.value: base_wage_or_salary

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Thereafter rates 6-1-04 6-1-05 6-1-06 6-1-07... selling and non-selling classifications.
evidence.heading: SECTION 33 - WAGE RATES AND JOB CLASSIFICATIONS

fields.coverage_check: Examples only; central table extraction needed for full vector.

scoreability.reason: Base wage rates are CBA-contained but require central normalization by classification, date, geography, hours, commissions and sector.

bridge_score.explanation: Wage scoring withheld by protocol.

review_notes: No local wage score; table is multi-page/classification-specific.

CBA_07.concept_label: Wage progression, steps, and classification movement
CBA_07.covered_group: New hires after ratification in all classifications under the CBA, with seasonal and experienced-hire variants.

excluded_groups: seasonal employees do not receive progressive wage increases during seasonal employment

subgroup_terms.terms: May be hired at progression step equivalent to comparable experience but not highest rate until 50 working days, subject to limited employer exception.
May be hired at 70% of thereafter rate and do not receive progressive wage increases during seasonal employment.

granularity_decision.reason: Progression percentages, experienced-hire placement and seasonal exceptions are one wage-progression object.

fields.value: wage_progression

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Progression schedule shall be: in hire 80%; 12 months 85%; 24 months 90%; 36 months 95%; 48 months thereafter rate.
evidence.heading: SECTION 33(B)

evidence.quote_or_pointer: In hire 80%... 12 months 85%... 24 months 90%... 36 months 95%... 48 months Thereafter rate.
evidence.heading: SECTION 33(B)

scoreability.reason: Progression parameters are supported but require central wage normalization and comparison by occupation/classification/date.

bridge_score.explanation: Wage progression score withheld by protocol.

review_notes: Seasonal 70% is progression-related exception, not base wage score.

CBA_07.concept_label: Wage increases, COLA, and lump-sum wage adjustments
CBA_07.covered_group: Employees at or above thereafter rates in specified classifications; all employees on roll for ratification bonus.

population_scope.scope_label: classification/date-specific wage increases and lump sum

subgroup_terms.subgroup_label: employees at or above thereafter rate
subgroup_terms.terms: Receive $0.40/hour in 2004, 2005, 2006 and $0.45/hour in 2007 in specified wage sections.
Ratification bonus of $250.

variant_terms.terms: 2006/2007 increase or bonus raised by $0.10 if sales exceed prior-year annual sales by 5% and customer service scores average 80.

subgroup_terms.terms: University health plan contributions continue for unit employees affected by those conditions lasting up to 4 months, subject to compliance with plan documents and rules.

CBA_17.concept_label: Parental, family, maternity, adoption, and FMLA-type leave
CBA_17.covered_group: pregnant, child-care, and adoptive teachers subject to stated eligibility

Further unpaid leave up to one year may be granted if pregnancy-related illness prevents return after maternity leave.

fields.value: without pay

evidence.quote_or_pointer: Article 35-1 states maternity leave is without pay; Article 35-6 further pregnancy-illness leave is without pay.

fields.semantic_role_check: Paid status is explicitly unpaid for stated leave.

review_notes: Scored only on CBA-contained contractual leave, not modern statutory baselines.
Subgroup limits are preserved.

CBA_17.concept_label: Personal, educational, union, political, religious, sabbatical, and other named leave

Shared-bank conversion flagged as non-additive.

CBA_17.concept_label: Benefit continuation during layoff, closure, leave, or disability

fields.semantic_role_check: This is an expedited arbitration timetable, not a filing deadline for a benefit.
fields.value: Special representatives must meet quickly; arbitrator deadlines are 72 hours in the early period and 24 hours thereafter.

granularity_decision.reason: The MOA creates several arbitration tracks with the same basic arbitration logic, so they belong in one arbitration record with distinct tracks noted as variants.

population_scope.scope_basis: The MOA creates several limited arbitration tracks for discipline cases, residual classifications, wage-rate cases, and organizing-rights disputes
population_scope.scope_label: Special-arbitration proceedings created by the MOA

review_notes: Keep the different arbitration tracks separate inside this one record.
The organizing-rights track should not be mistaken for a general grievance article.

missing_or_external_inputs: Any broader arbitration scope beyond the special tracks
scoreability.reason: The agreement creates several strong, neutral arbitration tracks, but they are specialized and spread across multiple attachments rather than a single general grievance system.

subgroup_terms.terms: Pilot project applies only to discipline cases; employers may join voluntarily; hearings should be expedited.
A permanent panel of six arbitrators hears rates for new or substantially modified jobs.
Standing panel of at least five arbitrators; shared costs; final and binding award.
Special arbitrator resolves campaign-rule disputes under expedited deadlines.

bridge_score.explanation: The union receives meaningful access, meeting space, speech protections, and procedural support during organizing, which is a strong access package even though it is limited to the campaign context.

HELDOUT_05.concept_label: Steward access, union business time, bulletin boards, and meeting rights
HELDOUT_05.covered_group: Union officers, organizers, delegates, and bargaining-unit employees in the identified units

evidence.heading: Union Organizing Rights
evidence.quote_or_pointer: The Employer shall allow access to the employee cafeteria and a suitable meeting room.

fields.semantic_role_check: This is an access right, not a wage or leave entitlement.
fields.value: Employer must allow access to the employee cafeteria and a suitable meeting room within four working days after notice.

evidence.heading: Union Organizing Rights
evidence.quote_or_pointer: The Employer shall not hold any mandatory one-on-one or group meetings with employees, a subject of which is representation by the Union.

fields.semantic_role_check: These are organizing-access and campaign-contact rules, not generic discipline rules.
fields.value: Employer and union campaign materials must be factual; mandatory meetings on the union-representation subject are barred; employees may distribute literature in non-working time and non-patient care areas.

evidence.heading: Contract and Benefits Administration Program
evidence.quote_or_pointer: The League and the Union have agreed to implement an employee released time program to provide for labor relations and benefits administration.

fields.semantic_role_check: This is union-business time context, not a wage or health plan term.
fields.value: Employee released-time program for labor relations and benefits administration.

granularity_decision.reason: The organizing access rights and the released-time program are related union-business objects, so they can live in one access record with a clear distinction between campaign access and administration release time.

population_scope.scope_basis: The organizing-rights provisions give the union and employees access during the campaign window; the released-time program is for labor-relations and benefits administration
population_scope.scope_label: Union officers, organizers, delegates, and bargaining-unit employees in the identified units

review_notes: Do not confuse the organizing campaign rules with the separate arbitration track.
The released-time program is incomplete and should not be overread as paid union leave.

missing_or_external_inputs: Paid access or paid steward time detail
scoreability.reason: The organizing-access package is strong and enforceable, but it is campaign-specific and not a paid union-business entitlement.

subgroup_terms.terms: May use cafeteria access and a suitable meeting room for organizing meetings.

subgroup_terms.subgroup_label: Employees in identified units or classifications
subgroup_terms.terms: May access the meeting room during non-working time and receive campaign information.

variant_terms.terms: Access rights apply during the organizing notice/election period.
Employee released-time program for labor relations and benefits administration within 30 days of ratification.
